channel.title: Muffins on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Muffins on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: Yogurt Blueberry Muffins
item.description:
Submitted by Ruth Divver

Aunt of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 cup plain yogurt
  • 2 tsp baking powder
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • ½ cup oil
  • 1 egg
  • 1 cup bran
  • 2 cups flour
  • ½ tsp vanilla
  • 1 cup blueberries

Instructions

In a small bowl, mix yogurt andoneteaspoon of baking powder. Set aside.

In a large bowl, mix all other ingredients, including the second teaspoon of baking powder. Mix the yogurt mixture into the large bowl.

item.title: Rhubarb Muffins
item.description:
Submitted by Marie and Will Sayer

Cousins of the Bride

  • 3 cups rhubarb, diced
  • 1 1/3 cups whole wheat flour
  • 1 1/3 cups all purpose flour
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1/4 tsp salt
  • 1 cup yogurt or sour cream
  • 1/2 cup vegetable oil
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 cup chopped nuts

Topping:

Ingredients

  • 1/2 cup brown sugar
  • 1/2 cup chopped nuts
  • 2 tbsp butter
  • 1 tsp cinnamon

Instructions

In a large bowl, combine rhubarb, flours, brown sugar, baking soda and salt.

item.title: Applesauce Oatmeal Muffins
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup all-purpose flour
  • ½ cup whole wheat flour
  • 3 tsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp cinnamon
  • 1/4 tsp nutmeg
  • ¾ cup rolled oats
  • ¼ cup Brown Sugar Twin
  • ½ cup raisins or ½ cup carob chips(optional)
  • 1 egg ¼ cup oil
  • 1/3 cup milk
  • 2/3 cup applesauce

Instructions

Mix dry ingredients well with a fork.

Beat egg, then add oil and milk.

Stir in applesauce.

Stir this into the dry ingredients, mixing only until moistened.

item.title: Applesauce Bran Muffins
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup All-Bran (Cereal)
  • ¼ cup milk
  • 1 cup applesauce
  • 1/3 cup oil
  • 1 egg
  • ½ cup raisins or ½ cup carob chips(optional)
  • ¾ cup all-purpose flour
  • ¾ cup whole wheat flour
  • 3 tsp baking powder
  • ½ tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • 1/3 cup Brown Sugar Twin

Instructions

Stir All-Bran, milk, applesauce, oil and egg together.

Stir together the dry ingredients.

Add bran mixture to dry ingredients, stirring just to moisten.

item.title: Strawberry Jam Muffins
item.description:
Yield: 24 muffins Baking Time: 20-25 minutes

Preparation Time: 15 minutes Oven Temperature: 350 degrees

Ingredients

  • 1 cup sugar
  • ¾ cup margarine
  • 3 eggs
  • 1 tsp soda
  • 2 cups sifted flour
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • 1 tsp nutmeg
  • 1/3 cup sour milk
  • ¾ cup jam

Instructions

Cream sugar and margarine. Add eggs one at a time, beating after each addition.

Sift dry ingredients together. Add dry ingredients alternately with sour milk. Fold in ¾ cup jam. Fill muffin tins ¾ full (grease if not using liners).

item.title: Carrot Cake Muffins
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 medium-sized carrots, peeled & grated
  • ½ tsp salt
  • ½ cup raisins
  • ¾ tsp cinnamon
  • ½ cup healthy margarine
  • ¼ tsp each ground ginger, nutmeg, allspice
  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • ¾ cup dark brown sugar
  • 1 cup whole wheat flour
  • 1 egg
  • 2-1/2 tsp baking powder
  • ½ cup buttermilk
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Instructions

  1.  Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Coat 12 muffin cups with a little margarine. Measure 1 cup grated carrot into a bowl. Add raisins. Melt ½ cup margarine in microwave or saucepan. Let cool to room temperature.

  2.  Place flours, then baking powder, soda, salt and spices into large bowl. Add sugar and stir with a fork until evenly blended. Make a well in center. Place egg, buttermilk and vanilla in another bowl. Whisk with a fork. Whisk in cooled margarine. Pour into well. Stir just until dry ingredients are moistened. Add carrots and raisins. Stir until evenly distributed.

  3.  Immediately spoon into muffin cups. Do not press down.

  4.  Bake in center of oven until a skewer inserted in center of muffin comes out almost clean, 17 to 20 minutes. Cool on a rack. Makes 12 muffins.

1 muffin – 235 Calories, 4 gr Proteins, 9 gr Fat, 36 gr Carbohydrate

item.title: Cheese and Onion Muffins
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 Tbsp baking powder
  • 1 Tbsp white sugar
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 1 cup shredded old cheddar cheese
  • 1 pkg onion soup mix
  • 1 cup milk
  • 1 egg
  • ¼ cup oil

Instructions

Stir together dry ingredients and stir in the cheese

Combine soup mix and milk. Let stand 5 min.

Beat egg, milk mixture and oil and add to dry ingredients mixing lightly just to combine.

item.title: Bran Muffins
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ¼ cup shortening
  • 1/2 cup firmly packed brown sugar
  • 1/4 cup molasses
  • 2 eggs, unbeaten
  • 1 cup milk
  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • 1-1/2 tsp baking powder
  • 1/2 tsp baking soda
  • ¾ tsp salt
  • 1-1/2 cup Quaker Natural Wheat Bran (Green Box)
  • 1/2 cup raisins

Instructions

Cream shortening and sugar together. Add molasses and eggs and beat together well. Add milk and then bran. Combine flour, baking power, soda and salt together. Add to liquid ingredients. Add raisins if desired. Place in greased muffin pans. Bake in 400 degree oven for l8 – 20 minutes. Makes l dozen large muffins.

item.title: Banana Muffins
item.description:
Preheat oven to 400 degrees. Blend or sift together:

  • 2 cups all-purpose flour
  • ¼ cup sugar
  • 4 tsp baking powder
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • Chopped walnuts (optional)

Make a well in center of the dry ingredients.

Combine and add:

Ingredients

  • 1 beaten egg
  • 2/3 cup milk
  • 1 cup well-mashed bananas
  • 2 Tbsp vegetable oil

Instructions

Stir only until combined. (The batter should be lumpy.) Fill greased muffin cups 2/3 full.

Bake in preheated oven for approx. 20 minutes.

item.title: All-Bran Muffins - No. 2
item.description:
Combine together:

  • 2 beaten eggs
  • 2 cups buttermilk
  • 2 cups brown sugar
  • 2 cups All-Bran (coarse)
  • 1 cup Mazola Oil
  • 1 tsp vanilla

Sift together, then add to the above:

Ingredients

  • 2 cups flour
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 2 tsp baking soda
  • 1 cup raisins
  • 2 tsp baking powder

Instructions

Fill medium-sized paper cups ¾ full. Bake for 15 to 20 minutes in a 375 degree oven until golden brown.

BB - I have no idea why this recipe has “No. 2” in its name. Likely the 2nd iteration of this, or just the 2nd Bran Muffin recipe she had. Regardless, I thought it was funny and topical with the type of recipe, so I left it in.

item.title: Bran Muffins
item.description:
Credit for this recipe goes to Clara Therrien

Ingredients

  • 1 cup All-Bran
  • ½ cup sugar
  • ½ cup raisins
  • 2 tsp baking powder
  • 1 cup milk
  • 1 cup flour
  • ½ cup butter
  • Pinch salt
  • 1 egg

Instructions

Mix all together and put into muffin tin. Bake at 400 F. for 15 to 20 minutes.

item.title: Chocolate Chip Banana Muffins
item.description:
Dry Mixture:

  • 2 cups flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1/2 cup chocolate chips

Wet Ingredients:

  • 1/2 cup margarine
  • 3/4 cup white sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1 cup ripe bananas, mashed

Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit and prepare muffin cups. Mix flour, baking soda, and salt. Stir in chocolate chips. Cream together margarine and sugar with electric mixer. Beat in eggs 1 at a time. Mash bananas and add bananas and vanilla to wet ingredients. Add wet ingredients to dry mixture all at once. Stir (do not use mixer) until mixture is moist but not lumpy. Fill muffin cups 3/4 full with batter. Bake 25 minutes at 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Yield: 12 muffins.

item.title: Easy Moist Banana Blueberry Muffins
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 large ripe bananas
  • 3/4 cup of sugar
  • 1 egg, slightly beaten
  • 1/3 cup of melted butter
  • 1 cup of blueberries
  • 1 tsp of baking soda
  • 1 tsp of baking powder
  • 1/2 tsp of salt
  • 1 1/2 cups of flour

Instructions

  • Mash bananas in a large mixing bowl.
  • Add sugar and egg.
  • Add butter and blueberries.
  • Combine dry ingredients and gently stir into banana mixture.
  • Pour into 12 well-greased muffin cups.
  • Bake at 375 degrees for 20 minutes.

Credit for this recipe goes to Food.com:
